text: Camouflaged Object Detection via Deep Gradient Network Camouflaged object detection (COD) is a newly emerging task that focuses on detecting objects rendered in a camouflage style, where objects and their surrounding environment exhibit a strong visual consistency. Most existing studies on COD rely on the annotation of object locations and the corresponding masks. However, such strong supervisory signals are often time-consuming and labor-intensive to obtain. In this work, we propose a Deep Gradient Network (DGNet) for camouflaged object detection by utilizing the object gradient supervision. The proposed DGNet is a simple yet effective deep learning framework. It consists of a context encoder and a texture encoder, which are connected by a gradient-induced transition mechanism for a “soft grouping” of contextual and textural features, facilitating the detection of camouflaged objects. Experimental results show that DGNet always achieves the best performance on three widely used COD benchmarks. Even when removing the semantic feature refinement component in DGNet, the remaining streamlined version, namely DGNet-S, can still maintain a real-time speed of 80 fps and surpasses the state-of-the-art JCSOD-CVPR21 model by a large margin with only 6.82% parameters. In addition, the proposed DGNet also shows robust performance in downstream tasks such as polyp segmentation, industrial defect detection, and transparent object segmentation. Our code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/GewelsJI/DGNet.
